message.content: verifique o conteúdo do arquivo src/screens/MyOrdersDetails/view.tsx, nele temos o inicio da implementacao do SDUI, porem estamos formatando os dados do lado do frontend por enquanto, continuaremos assim. Na lina 324 estamos mapeando e renderizando o unico componente já incluso nos UIElements, o KeyValueRow, porem precisamos substituir pelo componente que irá renderizar todos os tipos de elementos registrados.  da linha 258 até a 329, substitua tudo por um component que receba uma lista de UIElements e os renderize. pelo que vejo o KeyValueRow renderiza a maioria, faltando incluir o TagLabel nos UIElements e criar o component que recebe lista e renderiza todos. Crie, respeitando o style guide. o component que recebe lista deve ficar em organismes e as tipagens em ui.mode.ts, etc... Todos os mapeamentos devem seguir o exemplo já existente em orderDetailElements.mapper.ts

1→# Frontend Style Guide — Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2→
     3→Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4→This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5→
     6→> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ☕
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11→
    12→- [Introduction](#introduction)
    13→- [Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    14→- [Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    15→- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    16→- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    17→- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    18→- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    19→- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    20→- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    21→- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    22→- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    23→- [Checklist](#checklist)
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→## Introduction
    28→
    29→The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
    30→
    31→Key decisions:
    32→
    33→- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    34→- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    35→- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    36→- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    37→
    38→---
    39→
    40→## Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern
    41→
    42→Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    43→
    44→```
    45→screens/Home/
    46→├── index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    47→├── view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    48→└── Models/
    49→    └── index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    50→```
    51→
    52→**Models/index.d.ts**
    53→
    54→```ts
    55→export interface HomeParams {
    56→  welcome?: string;
    57→}
    58→export interface HomeState {
    59→  greeting: string;
    60→  isLoading: boolean;
    61→}
    62→export interface HomeHandlers {
    63→  onPressCTA: () => void;
    64→}
    65→export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    66→```
    67→
    68→**index.ts (logic hook)**
    69→
    70→```ts
    71→import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from 'react';
    72→import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from './Models';
    73→import { useUserStore } from '@/services/user/store'; // Zustand slice example
    74→
    75→export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    76→  const name = useUserStore(s => s.name);
    77→  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    78→
    79→  useEffect(() => {
    80→    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    81→    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    82→  }, []);
    83→
    84→  const greeting = useMemo(
    85→    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || 'guest'}!`,
    86→    [
    87→      params?.welcome,
    88→      name,
    89→    ],
    90→  );
    91→
    92→  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    93→    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    94→  }, []);
    95→
    96→  return {
    97→    greeting,
    98→    isLoading,
    99→    onPressCTA,
   100→  };
   101→}
   102→```
   103→
   104→**view.tsx (render)**
   105→
   106→```tsx
   107→import React from 'react';
   108→import { View } from 'react-native';
   109→import { Text, Button } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
   110→import { useHomeScreen } from './index';
   111→import type { HomeParams } from './Models';
   112→
   113→type Props = HomeParams;
   114→
   115→export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
   116→  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
   117→
   118→  if (isLoading) {
   119→    return (
   120→      <View>
   121→        <Text>Loading...</Text>
   122→      </View>
   123→    );
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  return (
   127→    <View>
   128→      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
   129→      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
   130→    </View>
   131→  );
   132→}
   133→```
   134→
   135→---
   136→
   137→## Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern
   138→
   139→```
   140→atomic/atoms/Button/
   141→├── index.ts        # logic: export useButton()
   142→├── view.tsx        # render: export default Button
   143→└── Models/
   144→    └── index.d.ts
   145→```
   146→
   147→**Models/index.d.ts**
   148→
   149→```ts
   150→export interface ButtonProps {
   151→  text: string;
   152→  disabled?: boolean;
   153→  onPress?: () => void;
   154→}
   155→export interface ButtonState {
   156→  pressed: boolean;
   157→}
   158→export interface ButtonHandlers {
   159→  handlePress: () => void;
   160→}
   161→export type UseButtonReturn = ButtonState & ButtonHandlers;
   162→```
   163→
   164→**index.ts (logic hook)**
   165→
   166→```ts
   167→import { useCallback, useState } from 'react';
   168→import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from './Models';
   169→
   170→export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
   171→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
   172→
   173→  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
   174→    setPressed(true);
   175→    props.onPress?.();
   176→    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
   177→  }, [props.onPress]);
   178→
   179→  return {
   180→    pressed,
   181→    handlePress,
   182→  };
   183→}
   184→```
   185→
   186→**view.tsx (render)**
   187→
   188→```tsx
   189→import React from 'react';
   190→import { Pressable, View } from 'react-native';
   191→import { Text } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
   192→import { useButton } from './index';
   193→import type { ButtonProps } from './Models';
   194→
   195→export default function Button(props: ButtonProps) {
   196→  const { pressed, handlePress } = useButton(props);
   197→
   198→  return (
   199→    <Pressable disabled={props.disabled} onPress={handlePress}>
   200→      <View>
   201→        <Text>
   202→          {props.text}
   203→          {pressed ? ' •' : ''}
   204→        </Text>
   205→      </View>
   206→    </Pressable>
   207→  );
   208→}
   209→```
   210→
   211→---
   212→
   213→## Project Organization
   214→
   215→```
   216→src/
   217→├── @types/            # Global TS types
   218→├── assets/            # Images, fonts, icons
   219→├── atomic/            # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
   220→│   ├── atoms/
   221→│   ├── molecules/
   222→│   └── organisms/
   223→├── modules/           # Microfrontends shared and native modules
   224→├── screens/           # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
   225→├── services/          # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
   226→│   ├── api/           # HTTP layer (Axios/Fetch)
   227→│   ├── class/         # Class with logical requests
   228→│   ├── hooks/         # Specific service hooks
   229→│   ├── repository/    # Data abstraction & mapping
   230→│   ├── services/      # Business orchestration
   231→│   ├── storage/       # Storage orchestration with cache or not
   232→│   ├── store/         # Zustand slices (only)
   233→│   ├── models/        # Domain types & contracts
   234→│   ├── utils/         # Reusable service helps
   235→│   └── index.ts       # Public exports
   236→├── hooks/             # Cross-cutting React hooks
   237→├── routes/            # Navigation config
   238→├── settings/          # Env & global config (axios, etc.)
   239→└── utils/             # General-purpose helpers
   240→```
   241→
   242→> UI **never** calls API directly. All effects & side effects live in the **logic hooks** (which call `/services`).
   243→
   244→---
   245→
   246→## Services Architecture
   247→
   248→**Flow:** `UI → services → repository → api` and `UI → services → store (Zustand)`
   249→
   250→- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.
   251→- **class/**: class with logical requests.
   252→- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
   253→- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.
   254→- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.
   255→- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.
   256→- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).
   257→- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.
   258→- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
   259→
   260→Principles:
   261→
   262→- Single responsibility per folder/file.
   263→- No circular dependencies.
   264→- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
   265→
   266→---
   267→
   268→## State Management (Zustand-only)
   269→
   270→- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.
   271→- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.
   272→- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.
   273→- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
   274→
   275→**Slice example**
   276→
   277→```ts
   278→import type { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
   279→import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from '../models/store';
   280→
   281→export type AuthSlice = {
   282→  auth: AuthState;
   283→  authActions: AuthActions;
   284→};
   285→
   286→const initialAuth = {
   287→  user: null,
   288→  loading: false,
   289→  error: undefined,
   290→};
   291→
   292→export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<
   293→  AuthSlice,
   294→  [],
   295→  [],
   296→  AuthSlice
   297→> = set => ({
   298→  auth: initialAuth,
   299→  authActions: {
   300→    setLoading: v =>
   301→      set(s => ({
   302→        auth: {
   303→          ...s.auth,
   304→          isLoading: v,
   305→        },
   306→      })),
   307→    setUser: v =>
   308→      set(s => ({
   309→        auth: {
   310→          ...s.auth,
   311→          user: v,
   312→        },
   313→      })),
   314→    setError: v =>
   315→      set(s => ({
   316→        auth: {
   317→          ...s.auth,
   318→          error: v,
   319→        },
   320→      })),
   321→    reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
   322→  },
   323→});
   324→```
   325→
   326→**Root Store**
   327→
   328→- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps — including SuperApp.
   329→
   330→```ts
   331→import { create } from 'zustand';
   332→import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from 'zustand/middleware';
   333→import { immer } from 'zustand/middleware/immer';
   334→
   335→import {
   336→  createUserSlice,
   337→  type UserSlice,
   338→} from '@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user';
   339→import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from '@semparar/mf-app-yourmf';
   340→
   341→import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
   342→
   343→/** Add your slice Type */
   344→export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
   345→
   346→export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
   347→  devtools(
   348→    persist(
   349→      immer((...a) => ({
   350→        ...createUserSlice(...a),
   351→        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
   352→      })),
   353→      {
   354→        name: 'root-storage',
   355→        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
   356→        partialize: s => ({
   357→          user: s.user,
   358→          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
   359→        }),
   360→        merge: (persisted, current) => ({
   361→          ...current,
   362→          ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>),
   363→        }),
   364→      },
   365→    ),
   366→    { name: 'RootStore' },
   367→  ),
   368→);
   369→```
   370→
   371→- For Use on Class.
   372→
   373→```ts
   374→class Auth {
   375→  private readonly setAuth;
   376→
   377→  constructor () {
   378→    this.setUser = useRootStore.getState().authActions.setUser;
   379→  }
   380→
   381→  function doSomething(data) {
   382→    this.setUser({ ...data });
   383→  }
   384→}
   385→```
   386→
   387→- For Use on Component/Screen.
   388→- UI never imports the store directly — uses selectors
   389→
   390→```ts
   391→const loading = useRootStore(s => s.user.isLoading);
   392→const token = useRootStore(s => s.auth.token);
   393→```

   415→## Code Standards
   416→
   417→**Language**: English only.  
   418→**Naming**: `camelCase` (vars/functions), `PascalCase` (components/classes), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (constants).

   427→isNumber(1);

   508→
   509→## Cache Pattern
   510→
   511→Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
   512→
   513→```ts
   514→import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
   515→
   516→export interface CachedData<T> {
   517→  timestamp: number;
   518→  data: T;
   519→}
   520→
   521→export interface StorageContract<T> {
   522→  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
   523→  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
   524→  remove(): Promise<void>;
   525→}
   526→
   527→export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
   528→  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
   529→
   530→  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
   531→    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
   532→    if (!json) return null;
   533→    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
   534→  }
   535→
   536→  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
   537→    const payload: CachedData<T> = {
   538→      timestamp: Date.now(),
   539→      data,
   540→    };
   541→    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
   542→  }
   543→
   544→  async remove(): Promise<void> {
   545→    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
   546→  }
   547→}
   548→```
   549→
   550→**Guidelines**
   551→
   552→- One storage class per module/feature.
   553→- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.
   554→- Use `timestamp` for freshness/expiry strategies.
   555→- Never persist secrets/PII outside secure storage.
   556→- Keep payloads small and JSON-serializable.
   557→

# JE0X7GHV [P0] Multiple fixes and improvements

- [DONE] FIX JSON Editor "Unknown component type" displaying in the wrong (next?)
json item.
  > Root cause: findJsonPath() in sduiLinter.ts had flawed array index traversal
  >   that failed for indices >= 2. The currentIdx counter logic couldn't properly
  >   track nested objects, causing null returns → error markers at position 0.
  > Fix: Replaced with skipValue()/skipWhitespace() helpers that properly skip
  >   complete JSON values (objects, arrays, strings, primitives) to reach target index.
  > Also fixed: string escape EOF edge case, added JSON5 single-quote support in skipValue.
  > All 25 existing linter tests pass. Storybook screenshot validated.

- [DONE] [P0] JSON Editor show suggestions also with option+space; currently it
shows with ctrl+space
  > Added Alt-Space keybinding in CodeMirrorEditor.tsx importing startCompletion
  >   from @codemirror/autocomplete and mapping { key: 'Alt-Space', run: startCompletion }.
  > Ctrl+Space remains the primary trigger (from completionKeymap).
  > Playwright validated: autocomplete tooltip appears on Alt+Space inside type value context.
  > Note: Alt-Space may conflict with OS window manager on Windows/Linux — acceptable
  >   since this is a macOS-primary Storybook dev tool.

- [DONE] [P0] FIX Unknown component: "nav_header" ‣ "Voucher para abastecimento",
pn storybook it must show a "mock" navbar, simulating the native app navbar
  > Created NavHeaderAdapter at src/services/sdui/adapters/NavHeaderAdapter.tsx.
  > Uses DS Header component with title and conditional back icon (icon-back).
  > Props: title (string, required), showBack (boolean, default true).
  > Registered as 'nav_header' in adapter registry (23 adapters total).
  > 3 unit tests added. All 33 adapter tests passing.
  > Replaces yellow FallbackComponent with proper DS Header across all SDUI stories.
  > onBack handler is stubbed (same pattern as LinkAdapter/ButtonAdapter — action
  >   handlers tracked separately in P1 task).
  > Screenshots validated: nav-header-voucher-form.png, nav-header-orderdetail.png.

- [DONE] [P1] Read all screens and plan what frontend action handlers will need to
implement from the frontend side (like link redirections, validations, sum
values, custom logics, etc.) in order to make all screen possible to be recreated
 using the SDUI engine then plan the architecture to handle it then map the
 handlers.
  > Analyzed all 11 screens (Points, Extract, InvoiceCashbackOnboarding,
  >   InvoiceCashbackPoints, InvoiceCashbackSummary, LearnMoreRewardProgram,
  >   MyOrdersRewardProgram, MyOrdersDetails, PointsDetail, AirlineMilesMarketplace,
  >   RewardProgramMarketplace).
  > Identified 7 action handler categories needed for full SDUI support.
  > Architecture plan documented below. Implementation tasks created as SP_0007.x.
  >
  > === SDUI ACTION HANDLER ARCHITECTURE PLAN ===
  >
  > PROBLEM: All SDUI adapters currently have stubbed `onPress={() => {}}`.
  > The engine renders UI but cannot execute any user-initiated actions.
  > To make screens fully SDUI-driven, the renderer must support a declarative
  > action system where JSON defines WHAT happens, and registered handlers
  > execute HOW it happens.
  >
  > DESIGN PRINCIPLE: Actions are declared in JSON, resolved at render time
  > via an ActionRegistry (similar to ComponentRegistry). The ScreenRenderer
  > passes an `onAction` callback to all adapters. Adapters call
  > `onAction(actionDescriptor)` instead of hardcoding behavior.
  >
  > --- ACTION DESCRIPTOR FORMAT ---
  > {
  >   "action": {
  >     "type": "navigate",          // handler key in ActionRegistry
  >     "payload": {                  // handler-specific data
  >       "screen": "InvoiceCashbackSummary",
  >       "params": { "points": "{{form.points}}" }
  >     }
  >   }
  > }
  >
  > --- 7 ACTION HANDLER CATEGORIES ---
  >
  > 1. NAVIGATION (navigate, goBack, replace, deepLink)
  >    - navigate: { screen, params? } → navigation.navigate()
  >    - goBack: {} → navigation.goBack()
  >    - deepLink: { url } → Linking.openURL() for "app://" and "tel:" URIs
  >    - replace: { screen, params? } → navigation.replace()
  >    Used by: nav_header (back), button (continue/confirm), link (rules/support)
  >
  > 2. API_CALL (fetch, submit, retry)
  >    - fetch: { service, method, params? } → call service method, update context
  >    - submit: { service, method, payload } → POST-like action (rescueRequest)
  >    - retry: { refetch: true } → re-invoke last failed fetch
  >    Used by: button (confirm rescue), error states (retry), pull-to-refresh
  >    NOTE: API calls update SDUI context (not Zustand) — the screen re-renders
  >    with new context values from the response.
  >
  > 3. FORM_STATE (setValue, setComputed, useAll)
  >    - setValue: { contextKey, value } → update form field in context
  >    - setComputed: { contextKey, formula } → compute derived value
  >      (e.g., points→reais conversion: { "*": [{ "var": "form.points" }, 0.01] })
  >    - useAll: { sourceKey, targetKey } → copy max value to input
  >    Used by: points_input (onChange), button ("Usar todos os pontos")
  >    NOTE: Form state lives in React useState in ScreenRenderer, merged
  >    with immutable backend context for interpolation. ScreenRenderer passes
  >    setForm callback in SDUIActionContext. NOT mutable ref (violates React
  >    unidirectional data flow and wouldn't trigger re-renders).
  >
  > 4. MODAL (open, close)
  >    - open: { modalId } → show modal/bottom sheet identified by ID
  >    - close: { modalId } → hide modal
  >    Used by: header help icon, "Relatar problema" button, filter buttons
  >    NOTE: Modals themselves are SDUI components declared in screen JSON
  >    under a "modals" array (new field). Modal visibility is context-driven.
  >
  > 5. ANALYTICS (trackScreenView, trackSelect, trackModalView)
  >    - trackScreenView: { screenName, params? }
  >    - trackSelect: { contentId, params? }
  >    - trackModalView: { modalName }
  >    Used by: every screen (view), every interactive component (select)
  >    NOTE: Analytics actions can be co-located with other actions via an
  >    "actions" array instead of single "action" object:
  >    { "actions": [{ "type": "navigate", ... }, { "type": "trackSelect", ... }] }
  >
  > 6. STORE_MUTATION (setState, reset)
  >    - setState: { slice, field, value } → zustand store update
  >    - reset: { slice } → reset slice to initial state
  >    Used by: MyOrders (clearFilters on goBack), Extract (reset on unmount)
  >    NOTE: Minimize store mutations from SDUI. Prefer context-driven state.
  >    Only use for cross-screen state that persists after navigation.
  >
  > 7. EXTERNAL (openURL, phoneCall)
  >    - openURL: { url } → Linking.openURL(url)
  >    - phoneCall: { number } → Linking.openURL('tel:' + number)
  >    Used by: support modal (tel:40021552, tel:08000150252), WebView screens
  >
  > --- ARCHITECTURE COMPONENTS ---
  >
  > A. SDUIAction type (models/index.ts)
  >    - SDUIActionDescriptor: { type: string, payload: Record<string, unknown> }
  >      + discriminated union for known types (navigate, goBack, setValue, etc.)
  >    - SDUIActionContext: { screen: SDUIContext, form: Record<string, unknown>,
  >      setForm: (updater) => void } — separates immutable backend data from
  >      mutable form state and provides React setState callback
  >    - SDUIActionHandler: (payload, actionContext, deps) => void | Promise<void>
  >    - SDUIActionRegistry: Record<string, SDUIActionHandler>
  >    - SDUIActionDeps: { navigation?, store?, linking?, analytics?, signal? }
  >    - SDUIActionResult: { success: boolean, error?: string, data?: unknown }
  >
  > B. ActionRegistry (actions/registry.ts)
  >    - createActionRegistry() → immutable registry
  >    - registerAction(type, handler) → register handler
  >    - executeAction(descriptor, actionContext, deps) → resolve and execute
  >    - executeActions(descriptors[], actionContext, deps, mode='sequential')
  >      → run multiple actions. Sequential by default. Analytics always fire
  >      even if primary action fails. Unknown types log warning + dev-only
  >      visual indicator.
  >    - Default registry with all 7 categories pre-registered
  >
  > C. ScreenRenderer update (renderer/ScreenRenderer.tsx)
  >    - Accept `onAction` prop (or actionRegistry + deps)
  >    - Pass `onAction` to every adapter via updated props interface
  >    - Form state: React useState, merged with screen.context via useMemo
  >    - AbortController for API call cleanup on unmount
  >    - Interpolate action payloads with merged context before executing
  >
  > D. Adapter updates (adapters/*.tsx)
  >    - ButtonAdapter: onPress → onAction(props.action)
  >    - LinkAdapter: onPress → onAction(props.action)
  >    - NavHeaderAdapter: onBack → onAction({ type: 'goBack' })
  >    - PointsInputAdapter: onChange → onAction({ type: 'setValue', ... })
  >    - All others: pass through onAction for interactive props
  >
  > --- SCREEN-BY-SCREEN ACTION MAPPING ---
  >
  > VOUCHER FORM (voucher_form):

  >   - link "Capitais" → { type: "phoneCall", payload: { number: "40021552" } }
  >   - link "Demais" → { type: "phoneCall", payload: { number: "08000150252" } }

  > EXTRATO (extract):

  >   - transaction_item onPress → { type: "navigate", payload: { screen: "PointsDetail", params: { transactionId: "{{item.id}}" } } }

# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
- [DONE] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.
  > src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts — evaluateRule() function with:
  >   var operator (via resolveContextPath), comparison ops (==, !=, >, <, >=, <=),
  >   logical ops (and, or, !), nested rule evaluation as operands.
  > Security: numeric type guard on ordering ops (>, <, >=, <=) rejects non-numbers,
  >   __proto__/constructor/prototype denylist added to resolveContextPath,
  >   MAX_DEPTH=20 recursion limit prevents stack overflow from malicious payloads.
  > ! operator handles both object and array syntax ({ "!": rule } and { "!": [rule] }).
  > 39 unit tests passing (jsonLogic.test.ts): var resolution, all 6 comparison ops,
  >   and/or/! logical ops, nested logic, edge cases (null/undefined/empty/unknown ops,
  >   non-numeric ordering, depth limit, prototype pollution guard).
  > Review fixes applied: BUG-3 (! array syntax), numeric type guards, depth limit,
  >   prototype pollution denylist, COMPARISON_OPS hoisted to module-scope Set.

Context:
- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
  and, or, ! (logical)
- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
  retornam false")
- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
- Must be pure function, no side effects
- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.

- Document form state persistence heuristic:
